domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Should details be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Details is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual details properties.
2. Details is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when details details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the details details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for details is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for details is enough.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: A single line of text for details is enough.

The domain description does not specify that details consist of multiple parts or require separate storage for filtering; it simply states that each assignment has details. Therefore, a single piece of text suffices for the purpose described.

questions.question:
Should the details in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use details as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use details as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grading, tutoring, course material preparation, lab assistance or administrative support.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The details can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The details must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The details can be any text value.

questions.question:
Should responsibilities be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Responsibilities is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual responsibilities properties.
2. Responsibilities is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when responsibilities details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the responsibilities details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for responsibilities is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for responsibilities is enough.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: A single line of text for responsibilities is enough.

The domain description does not specify that responsibilities consist of multiple parts or require detailed information; it simply states that each assignment has responsibilities. Therefore, a single line of text suffices for conveying this information.

questions.question:
Should the responsibilities in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use responsibilities as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use responsibilities as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grading, tutoring, course material preparation, lab assistance or administrative support.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The responsibilities can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The responsibilities must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 2: Enumeration responsibilities with predefined values: grading, tutoring, course material preparation, lab assistance and administrative support.

questions.question:
Does an assignment always require a course to exist. 
If a course is removed, would all the associated assignments also need to be removed?

For example:
1. An assignment belongs to a course and cannot exist on its own.
2. An assignment is linked to a course but can exist independently of it.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A assignment always belongs to a course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A assignment is linked to course but can exist independently.
questions.selected: Option 1: A assignment always belongs to a course.

questions.question:
In the context of a assignment, is it always associated with at least one course, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An assignment can exist without any course.
2. An assignment must have at least one course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An assignment can exist without any course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one course.
questions.selected: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one course.

questions.question:
In the context of a assignment, is it always associated with at least one teaching assistant, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An assignment can exist without any teaching assistant.
2. An assignment must have at least one teaching assistant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An assignment can exist without any teaching assistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one teaching assistant.
questions.selected: Option 2: An assignment must have at least one teaching assistant.

questions.question:
In the context of an assignment, can there be more than one teaching assistant involved, or is it always associated with just one? 
For example:
1. An assignment is linked to a single teaching assistant.
2. An assignment is linked to multiple teaching assistants.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each assignment is always linked to a single teaching assistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An assignment can be linked to multiple teaching assistants.
questions.selected: Option 2: An assignment can be linked to multiple teaching assistants.

questions.question:
In the context of a teaching assistant, is it always association with at least one assignment, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. A teaching assistant can exist without any assignment.
2. A teaching assistant must have at least one assignment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A teaching assistant can exist without any assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A teaching assistant must have at least one assignment.
questions.selected: Option 1: A teaching assistant can exist without any assignment.

questions.question:
In the context of a teaching assistant, can there be more than one assignment involved, or is it always associated with just one?
For example:
1. A teaching assistant is linked to a single assignment.
2. A teaching assistant is linked to multiple assignments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each teaching assistant is always linked to a single assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A teaching assistant can be linked to multiple assignments.
questions.selected: Option 2: A teaching assistant can be linked to multiple assignments.

questions.question:
In the context of a enrollment, is it always associated with at least one course, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An enrollment can exist without any course.
2. An enrollment must have at least one course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An enrollment can exist without any course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An enrollment must have at least one course.
questions.selected: Option 2: An enrollment must have at least one course.

For example:
1. A course can exist without any enrollment.
2. A course must have at least one enrollment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A course can exist without any enrollment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A course must have at least one enrollment.
questions.selected: Option 1: A course can exist without any enrollment.

questions.question:
In the context of a course, can there be more than one enrollment involved, or is it always associated with just one?
For example:
1. A course is linked to a single enrollment.
2. A course is linked to multiple enrollments.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each course is always linked to a single enrollment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A course can be linked to multiple enrollments.
questions.selected: Option 2: A course can be linked to multiple enrollments.

questions.question:
Does an enrollment always require a student to exist. 
If a student is removed, would all the associated enrollments also need to be removed?

For example:
1. An enrollment belongs to a student and cannot exist on its own.
2. An enrollment is linked to a student but can exist independently of it.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: A enrollment always belongs to a student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A enrollment is linked to student but can exist independently.
questions.selected: Option 1: A enrollment always belongs to a student.

questions.question:
In the context of a enrollment, is it always associated with at least one student, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An enrollment can exist without any student.
2. An enrollment must have at least one student.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An enrollment can exist without any student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An enrollment must have at least one student.
questions.selected: Option 2: An enrollment must have at least one student.

questions.question:
We know that each lab section has a time slot. When we think about time slot, is it just a simple piece of information, like a line of text? 
Or is time slot made up of different parts that might be important?
For example:
1. For each time slot, the start time and end time information is saved separately, so users can filter lab sections by start time and end time.
2. The time slot is a single piece of text. This works well when we just need to show the time slot.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the time slot details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for time slot is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: A single line of text for time slot is enough.

questions.question:
The concept enrollment potentially represents a link between courses and students. How should we understand this connection?

For example:
1. An enrollment is a concept and a link that connects a course and a student. 
2. An enrollment is a concept, but it does not represent a link between a course and a student.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The enrollment is a concept and a link between a course and a student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The enrollment does not represent a link between a course and a student.
questions.selected: Option 1: The enrollment is a concept and a link between a course and a student.

questions.question:
The concept assignment potentially represents a link between courses and teaching assistants. How should we understand this connection?

For example:
1. An assignment is a concept and a link that connects a course and a teaching assistant. 
2. An assignment is a concept, but it does not represent a link between a course and a teaching assistant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The assignment is a concept and a link between a course and a teaching assistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The assignment does not represent a link between a course and a teaching assistant.
questions.selected: Option 1: The assignment is a concept and a link between a course and a teaching assistant.
